vocab.example: Strategically placing a large mirror can significantly enhance the spatial perception of a cramped apartment.

vocab.meaning: tạo ảo giác về chiều sâu
vocab.example: I love using floor-to-ceiling mirrors because they create an illusion of depth in otherwise narrow hallways.

vocab.meaning: tối đa hóa ánh sáng tự nhiên
vocab.example: By positioning a mirror opposite the window, you can maximize natural light and brighten up the entire room.

vocab.meaning: khung gương chạm trổ công phu
vocab.example: An ornate frame can turn a simple mirror into a stunning focal point for a vintage-themed living room.

vocab.meaning: phong cách/sơ đồ thiết kế nội thất
vocab.example: Mirrors are versatile elements that fit seamlessly into almost any interior design scheme, from minimalist to industrial.

vocab.meaning: điểm nhấn/trung tâm sự chú ý
vocab.example: I decided to hang a sunburst mirror above the fireplace to serve as the main focal point of the area.

vocab.meaning: sự kết hợp giữa thẩm mỹ và công năng
vocab.example: Decorative mirrors offer a blend of aesthetic functionality, serving as both a piece of art and a practical tool.
I’m currently into mirrors with geometric patterns which add a contemporary and edgy vibe to my bedroom.

vocab.meaning: cạnh vát (được mài nghiêng để bắt sáng)
vocab.example: Mirrors with a bevelled edge possess a certain level of sophistication that flat mirrors lack.

vocab.meaning: làm rối mắt/choáng ngợp không gian thị giác
vocab.example: One should be careful not to overdo it with mirrors, as too many reflections can clutter the visual space.
